calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Mar-30-2026 Dismissal

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Nov-03-2025 Set For Trial On Mar-30-26 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
Off Calendar Sep-15-2025 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom Conference
JENNY TANG, 808-469-8198 jennytang23@gmail.com Pro Per Plaintiff.
No appearance by Plaintiff

Due to technicall issues with IJS the court's tenative ruling was provide by email after 3:00 p.m. by email. No notice of contest was received by parties. Per San Francisco Superior Court Local Rules 8.3(f) "If a tentative ruling is late, the parties must appear." Defendant contests the Court's tentative ruing.

Hearing held. discussed, not ARGUED. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT JENNY TANG MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS

Defendant Jenny Tang's motion to quash service of summons is denied. Tang has answered the complaint and thereby made a general appearance in the lawsuit. (Tang Dec. para. 6; Code Civ. Proc., section 1014 ["A defendant appears in an action when the defendant answers . . . .") Tang has accordingly waived any defect in service. (See Fireman's Fund Ins. Co. v. Sparks Construction, Inc. (2004) 114 Cal.App.4th 1135, 1145 ["A general appearance operates as a consent to jurisdiction of the person, dispensing with the requirement of service of process, and curing defects in service. . . . A general appearance can make up for a complete failure to serve a summons."] [citation simplified].)

Prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling.

Judge: Christine Van Aken: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/CVA)
